subject: 3.0.0 policy-handler - migrated to python 3.6
commit_message:
3.0.0 policy-handler - migrated to python 3.6

- migrated from python 2.7 to 3.6
- brought up the latest versions of dependencies
  -- Cherrypy 15.0.0, requests 2.18.4, websocket-client 0.48.0
- fixed migration errors
  -- renamed the standard package Queue to queue
  -- dict.items() instead of dict.iteritems()
  -- dict.keys() instead of dict.viewkeys()
  -- range() instead of xrange()
  -- subprocess.check_output(..., universal_newlines=True) to
     get str instead of byte-stream from stdout
- cleaned up migration warnings
  -- super() instead of super(A, self)
  -- logger.warning() instead of .warn()
- moved main() from policy_handler.py to __main__.py
- getting the policy_handler version directly from setup.py
  instead of the env var on init of the audit

Change-Id: I0fc4ddc51c08a64f3cfdc5d2f010b1c6a1ae92f0
Signed-off-by: Alex Shatov <alexs@att.com>
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-515

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Don't really know anything about python 2to3 migration, but changes look sane.

Patch Set 1:

Thank you for the comments

I will look into using the idea of "exec >> $LOG_FILE 2>&1" later on - sounds good - just would like to properly test that

Patch Set 1:

@Alex just want to bring to your attention the library called six http://six.readthedocs.io/ who's purpose is to make code both python 2 and python 3 compatible.  So instead of doing `dict.items()` you would do `six.items(dict())` and let six handle the nastiness.  Perhaps you don't care for python 2 so six won't matter.

Patch Set 1:

@Michael - thank you for pointing out to six.  I knew about that package.  However, I do not see a reason to keep the policy-handler compatible with python 2.7.  That would make the code less readable.  Plus, there are places like subprocess and super() that are harder/uglier to make happy in both py 2.7 and 3.6.  Plus, the policy-handler is inside the docker image, so the actual version of the python does not that matter anymore.  It would be easier to maintain only the latest one.
